title: Hologic, Inc. (HOLX) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Hologic, Inc. (HOLX) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1. Economic Prospect Score: 69/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 4.0% growth rate is estimated based on the recent free cash flow generation and the fundamental outlook for the Medical Instruments & Supplies sector.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A discount rate of 8.0% reflects the company's cost of capital, weighing systemic risks and consistent cash generation.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 2.0% is selected as the terminal growth rate, aligning with long-term macroeconomic GDP expectations.

thesisParagraphs: Hologic, Inc. currently generates stable free cash flows, which are critical for its valuation in the Medical Instruments & Supplies space. At a current price of $75.33, the market has embedded specific growth expectations into the stock.
Using a discounted cash flow model, we estimate an intrinsic value of $75.38. The current valuation suggests a margin of safety of 0.06%, leading to a FAIR VALUE verdict.
verdictFooter: Based on actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why was a 4.0% growth rate used?
faq.answer: The rate aligns with normalized historical and projected fundamental growth trends in the Medical Instruments & Supplies sector.

faq.question: What does the discount rate reflect?
faq.answer: The WACC represents the blended cost of equity and debt, capturing the risk profile of the cash flows.

faq.question: Is this financial advice?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is an AI-generated model based on quantitative inputs and rigid formulas.

prospectVerdictDetail: Hologic maintains a strong position in women's health diagnostics. Its consistent cash generation and dominant position in mammography equipment and molecular diagnostics provide a solid foundation for long-term compounding, resulting in a strong economic prospect.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Hologic shows solid competitive momentum, transitioning successfully from a capital equipment focus to a recurring revenue model led by its molecular diagnostics franchise.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: Growth is steady and largely in line with peers. The recurring revenue from test assays helps smooth out the lumpiness of capital equipment sales, driving mid-single-digit top-line growth.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: The company holds a dominant share in 3D mammography. Its massive installed base allows it to cross-sell additional tests, locking in its market position.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Hologic demonstrates good pricing power in its premium imaging systems, though standardized testing faces tighter reimbursement constraints from insurers.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Product iterations are steady, focusing on software enhancements and expanding the menu of assays available on its Panther system.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Hologic's moat is wide and durable, built on high switching costs for its installed base of complex diagnostic equipment and robust intellectual property.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: Once a laboratory installs a Panther system or a hospital trains its staff on Hologic mammography equipment, the financial and operational friction to switch vendors is exceptionally high.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: Network effects are less direct, but an increasing menu of validated tests on its platforms creates a strong ecosystem that attracts more lab volume.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: The company holds a vast portfolio of patents protecting its 3D imaging and molecular assay technologies, combined with the high hurdle rate of FDA approvals.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: Its razor-and-blades model—selling capital equipment once and generating high-margin recurring revenue from consumables—is highly capital efficient over the equipment lifecycle.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: Sentiment is constructive, reflecting confidence in management's execution and capital return strategy, though outsized growth surprises are unlikely.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Estimates are stable, reflecting the predictability of its recurring revenue streams. The market expects steady, methodical EPS growth largely aided by share repurchases.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: The narrative has transitioned from pandemic-era volatility to acknowledging Hologic as a reliable, defensive healthcare compounder with consistent cash flows.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management effectively utilizes robust free cash flow for systematic share repurchases and strategic, disciplined acquisitions that enhance the product portfolio.

prospectKeyRisks: Heavy reliance on women's health diagnostics creates concentration risk.
Changes in hospital capital expenditure budgets could delay equipment upgrades.
Pricing pressure from healthcare consolidation and reimbursement changes.
prospectKeyCatalysts: Continued adoption of higher-margin molecular diagnostic assays.
Expansion into international markets with its core 3D mammography systems.
Strategic tuck-in acquisitions to expand product portfolio and recurring revenues.
prospectMethodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored HOLX at 70/100 and Opus at 66/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
